approval_gates.requirement: A Taskmaster task and work-tracking folder explicitly name the cleanup target and allowed operation.
Scanner, duplicate, reference-fix, or command dry-run evidence exists before any mutation is proposed.
A backup, archive, or restore path is documented before deleting or rewriting generated artifacts.
A rollback checkpoint or recovery plan exists for any multi-file cleanup.
The user approves the exact cleanup command or patch after reviewing evidence.
Guard, tests, audit, Taskmaster health, and diff-check pass after cleanup.

backup_rollback_guidance: Capture `python3 scripts/codex-task rollback checkpoint` before risky multi-file cleanup.
Use `python3 scripts/codex-task rollback plan` to render recovery guidance from the checkpoint.
Prefer targeted `git restore -- <path>` only after review; never use `git reset --hard` as a default cleanup path.
Keep scanner backup directories until the related PR is merged and the archive commit is pushed.

cleanup_candidates.manual_action: Review generated duplicate/reference outputs and archive only explicitly approved stale artifacts.
cleanup_candidates.title: Scanner-generated cleanup artifacts

cleanup_candidates.manual_action: Review backup age and restore value before pruning or archiving backup directories.
cleanup_candidates.title: Reference-fix backup directories

cleanup_candidates.manual_action: Use deprecation review status before archive/removal decisions; create a scoped cleanup task for each accepted change.
cleanup_candidates.title: Deprecated template candidates

cleanup_candidates.manual_action: Reuse the Task 108 pattern: isolate one legacy finding, apply the smallest change, and verify scanners/guard.
cleanup_candidates.title: Legacy PROJECT-BLOG cleanup precedent

cleanup_candidates.manual_action: Never clear active session, plan, or work-tracking pointers except during post-merge archive closeout.
cleanup_candidates.title: Active workflow state cleanup

domains.message: Taskmaster cleanup health evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Confirm cleanup follow-up work can be tracked without dependency corruption.
domains.title: Taskmaster cleanup health

domains.message: Scanner cleanup evidence evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Use generated scanner/duplicate/reference data as cleanup candidate evidence.
domains.title: Scanner cleanup evidence

domains.message: Reference-fix safety tools evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Confirm dry-run, backup, and rollback tooling exists for reference cleanup.
domains.title: Reference-fix safety tools

domains.message: Deprecation lifecycle review evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Use lifecycle/deprecation evidence before deciding archive or removal candidates.
domains.title: Deprecation lifecycle review

domains.message: Rollback and emergency policy evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Require recovery context before risky cleanup work.
domains.title: Rollback and emergency policy

domains.message: Legacy cleanup example evidence is ready for cleanup planning.
domains.purpose: Ground cleanup planning in the scoped Task 108 legacy PROJECT-BLOG cleanup precedent.
domains.title: Legacy cleanup example

dry_run_checks.purpose: Confirm scanner command surface before using generated cleanup data.
Preview reference-fix candidates without writing files.
Confirm active/archive state before moving work-tracking folders.
Confirm cleanup planning does not break workflow evidence.

label: cleanup-automation
manual_notification_guidance: Use communication templates to draft cleanup notices when stakeholders need review.
Do not send notifications from this command; record draft or decision in work tracking.
Mention exact candidate IDs, evidence paths, and verification commands in any PR description.

metrics_checklist.review_question: How many cleanup candidates are proposed by scanner, lifecycle, or historical evidence?
How many candidates lack source evidence or dry-run output?
How many candidates are low, medium, high, or destructive-risk?
Do all candidates have scope, dry-run, backup, rollback, review, and verification gates?
Which candidates have rollback checkpoint or restore guidance?
Will guard, tests, audit, Taskmaster health, and diff-check be captured after cleanup?

non_goals: No cron job, scheduler, daemon, GitHub scheduled workflow, background cleanup service, or long-running process is installed.
No file is deleted, moved, archived, restored, rolled back, cleaned, reset, or modified by this command.
No `git clean`, `git reset`, destructive restore, branch deletion, retention enforcement, or automatic cleanup approval is executed.
No backup is created or restored; backup and rollback guidance is advisory only.
No email, chat, webhook, ticket, stakeholder notification, or external service call is sent.
No source report, scanner output, template, Taskmaster, session, plan, work-tracking, Git, or external state is mutated beyond requested cleanup planning artifacts.
planning_guidance: Treat this packet as a cleanup review contract; a human must approve every cleanup action separately.
Run dry-run scanners and capture rollback context before any cleanup implementation task.
Keep candidate rows advisory until an explicit Taskmaster task scopes the exact file operation.
Prefer archive or rewrite decisions over deletion when historical evidence may still be useful.
recommended_refresh_commands: python3 scripts/codex-task cleanup plan --report-file reports/cleanup-automation/latest.json --runbook-file reports/cleanup-automation/latest.md
